strategy: Bull Call Spread $60/$85 Aug 2026 — drone and hypersonics leader at 57% discount to 52-week high; IV ~50% leans spread; Strong Buy consensus with $96 avg target

wheelStrategy.wheelFlag: INSIDER SELLING NOTE: Multiple executives sold shares under pre-arranged 10b5-1 plans at $61–$77 in April–May 2026 — just above/around current price. 10b5-1 plans are pre-scheduled and not discretionary, but the concentration of sales in the $60–77 range is worth noting as a near-term sentiment signal.
wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX elevated ~20+; defense sector IV runs elevated given geopolitical uncertainty and earnings volatility. KTOS moved 5.3% down on Q1 despite a beat — options chain reflects this two-way risk.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: Lower Bollinger Band estimated ~$51–$53. Stock at $56.92 is near the lower band after the 57% pullback from its $134 52-week high. This is the Bollinger entry zone the methodology looks for.

csp.exitRule: Close at 50% premium captured. Hard stop before ~July 24 (5 days before est. Q2 2026 earnings in late July).
csp.note: The $52.50 CSP is 7.8% below current price — modest buffer given high beta. Enter only if stock confirms support above $54; do not enter into falling momentum.

coveredCall.exitRule: Close at 50% premium captured. Do not hold through earnings.
coveredCall.note: If assigned at $52.50, sell $60 CC to reduce cost basis to ~$50. Strong premium at current IV level.

expiryComparison.iv: ~50% (defense sector; elevated post-earnings volatility on large 52-week range stock)
expiryComparison.closeDeadline: July 24, 2026 (5 days before est. Q2 earnings)

exitRules.entryTrigger: Enter after stock stabilises above $56.00 on a closing basis. The 5.3% post-earnings drop despite a beat suggests sentiment is cautious — wait for the dust to settle before entering. Do not buy into falling momentum.
exitRules.profitExit: Close Contract 1 when spread reaches 50% of max gain (~$625 on $60/$85). Let Contract 2 run to $85 target if stock momentum supports. Consider partial exit at $70.
exitRules.timeStop: If stock has not broken above $63.00 by June 15 (50% of DTE), close the position. Defense stocks can drift sideways for extended periods; theta erodes quickly past mid-life.
exitRules.lossStop: Close at 40–50% loss on net debit paid (close at ~$1.50–$1.75 loss on $3.50 net paid). Budget uncertainty or a production setback could reprice sharply.
exitRules.earningsHardStop: Close ALL positions by July 24, 2026 — 5 days before estimated Q2 2026 earnings. KTOS moved 5.3% on Q1 and has a 52-week range of $32–$134. Q2 is volatile. No exceptions.

options.flatScenario: Stock stays at $56.92 — spread expires worthless at close deadline. Loss: ~$350 per spread.
options.atTarget: Stock at $85.00 by July 24 — spread worth ~$22.00–25.00. Gain: ~$1,850–$2,150 per spread (~540% return on $350).
options.bestFor: Optimal: 77 days working time, cheapest premium, best risk/reward at 5-6x max.

options.flatScenario: Same close deadline — paying $0.50 extra per spread for unused post-earnings time.
options.atTarget: Identical outcome at July 24 close. +$50/spread extra cost with no benefit.
options.bestFor: Only if Q2 earnings are pushed past August — date slippage insurance.

options.flatScenario: Paying $1.20 extra for time entirely cut off by the July 24 earnings hard stop.
options.atTarget: Same close outcome — Aug is strictly cheaper.
options.bestFor: Not recommended — thesis resolves at Q2 or before. Aug is optimal.

recommendation.title: Bull Call Spread — $60/$85 Aug 15, 2026
recommendation.type: Bull Call Spread (IV ~50% — 'either, lean spread' zone; $85 short leg saves $2.50 vs single call; defined risk sensible given cash flow and sentiment risk)
recommendation.entryNote: IV at ~50% puts us in the 'either — lean spread' zone per methodology. Selling the $85 call saves $2.50/contract and caps the position at the conservative $85 target (vs $96.71 analyst consensus). Max gain $2,150 per spread at $85+. The setup: KTOS is 57% below its $134 52-week high despite raising FY2026 guidance and reporting +22.6% revenue growth in Q1. Record $2B backlog, 1.6x book-to-bill, $447M Space Force contract, and hypersonics revenue growing from $400M (2026) toward $700M (2027). Stock fell 5.3% on the Q1 beat — investor concerns about negative operating cash flow ($-27.4M) and high capex are creating the entry. Enter on stabilisation above $56.

companyOverview: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions is a pure-play advanced defense technology company with three key franchises: (1) Unmanned Aerial Systems — the XQ-58A Valkyrie tactical drone is the crown jewel, a low-cost attritable jet drone designed for air-to-air combat alongside F-35s; production ramping to 40 units/year by 2028. (2) Hypersonic and satellite propulsion systems — $400M revenue expected in 2026, growing to $700M in 2027. (3) Space and satellite ground systems — a $447M prime Space Force contract (Resilient Missile Warning & Tracking) anchors this segment. Q1 2026 revenue hit $371M (+22.6% YoY) and FY2026 guidance was raised to $1.70–$1.76B (15–19% organic growth). Record $2.0B backlog and 1.6x book-to-bill confirm demand well above supply. The stock trades at $56.92 — 57% below its $134 52-week high — despite a strong fundamental setup, creating an asymmetric opportunity relative to the $96.71 analyst consensus target.

financials.note: Working capital build; receivables +$28.7M, inventory +$14.7M — a risk to watch
Book-to-bill 1.6x — demand accelerating ahead of supply
Growing to ~$700M in 2027; key margin driver

insiderFlow.summary: Multiple executives selling under pre-arranged 10b5-1 plans at $61–$77 — above current price but plans were set up when stock was much higher. Automated execution, not discretionary conviction sells.

transactions.note: Pre-arranged plan adopted May 20, 2025 (when stock was higher). Automated execution — not a conviction sell at current prices.
Sold at $72.67–$77.26 under a pre-arranged plan from June 2025. Stock has since fallen 23% from his sale price.
Pre-arranged plan. Sold near current levels — modest in size, not a material signal.

insiderFlow.signal: NEUTRAL-CAUTIOUS — all sales are under pre-arranged 10b5-1 plans (non-discretionary). However, the concentration of plan executions in the $61–$77 range, just above current price, is worth monitoring. No open-market discretionary purchases to offset. Watch for any fresh 10b5-1 plan filings at lower prices — that would be a positive signal.

catalysts: Valkyrie XQ-58A drone: the US Air Force's primary low-cost attritable tactical drone — production ramping to 40 units/year by 2028; international export demand building
Hypersonics: revenue growing from $400M (2026E) to $700M (2027E) — propulsion systems for next-generation missiles are a multi-year government priority
$447M Space Force prime contract (Resilient Missile Warning & Tracking) — new anchor contract for the space/satellite segment
Record $2.0B backlog + 1.6x book-to-bill: demand is running well ahead of capacity — revenue acceleration is structural
FY2026 guidance raised to $1.70–$1.76B after a strong Q1 beat — management has conservative track record
EBITDA margin guided +100bps YoY in both 2026 and 2027 — operating leverage kicking in as production scales
Stock at 57% discount to 52-week high despite fundamentals improving — sentiment vs. reality divergence
risks: Negative operating cash flow: Q1 FCF -$43.1M due to working capital build — if this pattern continues, liquidity risk emerges at scale
Stock fell 5.3% on a Q1 earnings beat — market pricing in budget uncertainty and execution risk, not the fundamental story
US defense budget risk: Congressional continuing resolutions or sequestration could delay contract awards and stretch timelines
Multiple 10b5-1 insider sales executing near current price ($61–$77 range) — psychological ceiling effect
Valkyrie production ramp execution: transitioning from low-rate to 40 units/year is a manufacturing scale challenge
52-week range $32.85–$134.00 shows extreme volatility — this stock can move 50%+ in either direction
dilution: Kratos has issued shares for acquisitions and compensation over its history. Current share count ~127M. Stock-based compensation is moderate relative to revenue scale. The company does not pay dividends and reinvests cash into growth — dilution risk is manageable but present as the company funds its production ramp.

technicalSetup.pattern: Deep pullback from 52-week high finding support
technicalSetup.description: KTOS fell 57% from $134 to $56.92 over several months while fundamentals improved. The stock is now testing the 2026 lower support zone near $54–$57. If this level holds, the setup mirrors a classic 'high-growth pullback to prior breakout' pattern with the recovery target near $75–$85.

keyLevels.note: Multi-month lows and lower Bollinger Band — must hold for bullish thesis
Zone where insiders sold under 10b5-1 plans — first major supply level
Conservative analyst target; spread max gain level
